title: pilosella tardiuscula subsp. tardiuscula
intro: Pilosella tardiuscula subsp. tardiuscula, also known as Hooded Ragwort, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various digestive issues and inflammation. The health benefits of Pilosella tardiuscula subsp. tardiuscula include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Pilosella tardiuscula subsp. tardiuscula can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, and can also be applied topically to the skin for external treatments.
